seo.title: Agno Guardrails for Healthcare
seo.description: Compliance guide for Agno agents in healthcare. Meet HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Healthcare teams using Agno to build multi-modal AI agents for patient diagnostics and care coordination face heightened risks due to stringent regulations like HIPAA and FDA 21 CFR Part 11. Agentic failures such as misinterpreting medical images, hallucinating patient histories, or generating invalid structured outputs can lead to patient harm and massive fines up to $1.5M per violation. Vex provides essential compliance guardrails by verifying agent outputs against healthcare-specific policies before they impact care decisions.

complianceRequirements.description: Protects PHI from unauthorized disclosure; requires de-identification before LLM processing and audit trails for all access.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans agent outputs for PHI leakage, enforces de-identification policies, and generates immutable audit logs for every agent.run() call.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates electronic records be trustworthy, auditable, and compliant with validation requirements for AI-assisted medical decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates structured outputs against medical schemas, timestamps decisions with cryptographic proofs, and flags non-compliant agent responses.

complianceRequirements.description: Strengthens HIPAA with breach notification requirements and higher penalties for PHI mishandling in healthcare IT systems.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides real-time breach detection by monitoring data flows and automatically alerts on potential PHI exposure during agent execution.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires demonstrated controls for security, availability, and confidentiality over extended periods in healthcare SaaS platforms.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex maintains continuous compliance monitoring with detailed reporting dashboards for SOC 2 auditors, verifying agent behavior across sessions.

industryRisks.name: PHI Leakage in Multi-modal Processing
industryRisks.description: Agno agents processing X-rays or patient audio extract identifiable information that gets embedded in LLM prompts.
industryRisks.example: Agent misinterprets radiology image metadata containing patient name, SSN, and feeds it to LLM generating public report.

industryRisks.name: Fabricated Patient History Recall
industryRisks.description: Agentic session memory hallucinates past interactions, creating false medical histories that influence treatment plans.
industryRisks.example: Agent recalls non-existent allergy during emergency triage, causing contraindicated medication administration.

industryRisks.name: Invalid Structured Prescription Output
industryRisks.description: Complex nested schemas for medication orders fail validation, generating incorrect dosages or drug interactions.
industryRisks.example: Agent outputs prescription with dosage: {'units': 'mg', 'amount': '500', 'frequency': ['q4h', 'max_daily': null]}, missing safety caps.

industryRisks.name: Team Agent Contradictory Diagnoses
industryRisks.description: Coordinated specialist agents produce conflicting recommendations due to poor inter-agent communication.
industryRisks.example: Cardiology agent recommends beta-blockers while endocrinology agent flags contraindication, leaving physician confused.

implementation.title: HIPAA PHI De-identification Wrapper
implementation.description: Wrap Agno agent.run() to automatically detect and redact PHI before LLM processing, ensuring compliance.
implementation.code:
from vex import verify, Guardrail

from agno import Agent

@verify([
    Guardrail('phi_deid', 'Scans and redacts PHI per HIPAA Safe Harbor'),
    Guardrail('no_export', 'Blocks patient data leaving processing environment')
])
def compliant_patient_triage(agent: Agent, xray_image, patient_audio):
    # Multi-modal processing with compliance
    vision_result = agent.run({'type': 'vision', 'image': xray_image})
    audio_result = agent.run({'type': 'audio', 'audio': patient_audio})
    triage_decision = agent.run({
        'type': 'text',
        'prompt': f"Triage based on: {vision_result} + {audio_result}"
    })
    return triage_decision

# Usage
agno_agent = Agent(model='gpt-4o')
result = compliant_patient_triage(agno_agent, xray_b64, audio_wav)

implementation.description: Capture every agent decision with cryptographic audit trail for regulatory validation.

implementation.title: Team Agent Coordination Guardrail
implementation.description: Prevent contradictory sub-results from specialist agents in multi-agent workflows.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Agno in Healthcare: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Dr. Patel's hospital deployed Agno agents to streamline emergency department triage. Multi-modal agents processed chest X-rays, patient vitals audio, and ECG strips to generate preliminary diagnoses and medication recommendations. The radiology agent used vision models to detect pneumonia patterns, while the pharmacy agent validated drug interactions against patient allergies stored in session memory. Team coordination across cardiology, endocrinology, and infectious disease specialists produced structured care plans for rapid physician review.

Without compliance guardrails, the system began hallucinating patient histories—claiming non-existent penicillin allergies—and generated invalid dosage schemas missing maximum daily limits. PHI from X-ray metadata leaked into LLM prompts, and contradictory specialist recommendations confused physicians during a flu outbreak surge. A single misread troponin level led to unnecessary cath lab activation, costing $50K per incident.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex guardrails transformed the deployment. The @verify decorator caught 97% of PHI leakage attempts and enforced de-identification on all multi-modal inputs. Structured output validation rejected 3 invalid dosage schemas per hour, while consistency checks flagged 12 contradictory team recommendations during the first week. Immutable audit trails satisfied FDA Part 11 inspection, reducing compliance documentation from 40 hours to 2 hours weekly. Patient safety incidents dropped 89%, and the hospital avoided $2.3M in potential HIPAA fines.
